questions.text: Every ________ person in India is poor.
questions.explanation: Roughly 270 million people in India live in poverty, meaning every fourth person is poor.

questions.text: Poverty as seen by social scientists involves which indicators?
options: Lack of access to safe drinking water
questions.explanation: Social scientists look at multiple indicators beyond just income and consumption.

questions.text: A common method used to measure poverty is based on:
questions.explanation: The poverty line is a threshold based on a minimum level of income or consumption.

questions.text: The accepted average calorie requirement in India is ________ calories per person per day in rural areas.
questions.explanation: People in rural areas engage in more physical work and thus require more calories.

questions.text: The accepted average calorie requirement in urban areas is ________ calories per person per day.
questions.explanation: Urban areas have a lower calorie requirement of 2100 due to less intense physical labour.

questions.text: Which organization carries out sample surveys for estimating the poverty line?
questions.explanation: The National Sample Survey Organisation (NSSO) conducts these surveys periodically.

questions.text: For making comparisons between developing countries, the World Bank uses a uniform standard for the poverty line as:
questions.explanation: The World Bank uses a consistent monetary value for international comparison.

questions.text: Social groups which are most vulnerable to poverty are:
questions.explanation: These groups face higher risks of falling and remaining below the poverty line.

questions.text: Within family, who are the 'poorest of the poor' as they are denied equal access to resources?
questions.explanation: Women, elderly, and female infants are often the most deprived within a poor household.

questions.text: Which of the following states has the highest poverty ratio in India?
questions.explanation: Bihar and Odisha continue to be the two poorest states in India.

questions.text: Which state has seen a significant decline in poverty due to land reform measures?
questions.explanation: Land reforms in West Bengal helped in reducing poverty by distributing resources more equitably.

questions.text: States like Punjab and Haryana have succeeded in reducing poverty because of:
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution and high farm output led to higher incomes in these states.

questions.text: Kerala has reduced poverty by focusing more on:
questions.explanation: Heavy investment in education and healthcare has improved productivity and income in Kerala.

questions.text: In which country did poverty decline from 88% in 1981 to 0.7% in 2018?
questions.explanation: China's rapid economic growth and investment in human capital led to massive poverty reduction.

questions.text: The main cause of poverty in India during the British rule was:
options: Low level of economic development under colonial administration
questions.explanation: British policies discouraged traditional handicrafts and industrial development.

questions.text: One of the major causes of poverty is the unequal distribution of:
questions.explanation: Landlessness is a major cause of poverty in rural India.

questions.text: The current anti-poverty strategy of the government is based on:
options: Promotion of economic growth and targeted anti-poverty programmes
questions.explanation: The government focuses on growing the overall economy while providing specific help to the poor.

questions.text: Which act aims to provide 100 days of wage employment to every household?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (2005) is a landmark scheme.

questions.text: What is the full form of MGNREGA?
options: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act
Minimum Government National Rural Employment Grant Act
Mahatma Gandhi National Road Employment Guarantee Act
Modified General National Rural Employment Guarantee Act
questions.explanation: It provides a legal guarantee for at least 100 days of manual work.

questions.text: Under MGNREGA, if an applicant is not provided employment within 15 days, they are entitled to:
questions.explanation: The act includes a provision for an allowance if the government fails to provide work.

questions.text: Prime Minister Rozgar Yozana (PMRY) was started in 1993 to:
options: Create self-employment opportunities for educated unemployed youth in rural areas/small towns
questions.explanation: PMRY helps educated youth start small businesses and industries.

questions.text: Rural Employment Generation Programme (REGP) was launched to create self-employment in:
questions.explanation: REGP targetted job creation within rural settings.

questions.text: Poverty line in India is higher in urban areas than rural areas because of:
options: Higher prices of essential items in cities
questions.explanation: Despite lower calorie needs, the cost of living is higher in urban areas.

questions.text: The proportion of poor people is not the same in every state. This is called:
questions.explanation: Some states have been much more successful than others in reducing poverty.

questions.text: Pradhan Mantri Gramodaya Yozana (PMGY) focuses on:
questions.explanation: PMGY provides additional central assistance to states for basic services.

questions.text: Poverty is not just a lack of money; it's also about ________ according to newer definitions.
questions.explanation: Concepts like 'Human Poverty' broaden the definition beyond mere income.

questions.text: Who is the poorest group among the vulnerable groups in India?
questions.explanation: Scheduled Tribes have the highest poverty ratio among the listed groups.

questions.text: In South East Asia, poverty has declined due to:
options: Rapid economic growth and investment in human resources
questions.explanation: Growth-oriented policies have uplifted millions in this region.

questions.text: Vulnerability to poverty is a measure which describes the probability of certain communities becoming:
questions.explanation: It determines how likely a group is to be affected by shocks like natural disasters.

questions.text: Social exclusion means that the poor are excluded from:
options: Enjoying social equality with better-off people
questions.explanation: It describes a situation where poor people have to live in a poor surrounding excluded from better facilities.

questions.text: Poverty line remains ________ over time and place.
questions.explanation: The poverty line changes depending on the time and the development level of the country.

questions.text: In 2011-12, the poverty line for a person was fixed at ________ per month for rural areas.
questions.explanation: This was the threshold set for rural consumption in that period.

questions.text: Poverty has ________ in Sub-Saharan Africa.
questions.explanation: Unlike China or India, poverty in parts of Africa has been very difficult to reduce.

questions.text: Which state has a high poverty ratio but is improving through public distribution of food grains?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu have effectively used PDS to reduce poverty.

questions.text: Massive ________ of population makes it difficult for economic growth to reduce poverty.
questions.explanation: High population growth rates can offset the gains made by economic development.

questions.text: Lack of ________ is a major reason for the failure of many anti-poverty schemes.
questions.explanation: Benefits often do not reach the intended poor due to administrative failures.

questions.text: SGSY stands for:
questions.explanation: SGSY aims at bringing assisted poor families above the poverty line by organizing them into self-help groups.

questions.text: Most poor people in urban areas are:
questions.explanation: Urban poor typically work in the unorganized sector with low and irregular wages.

questions.text: Which of these is not a cause of poverty in India?
questions.explanation: High literacy is actually a solution to poverty, not a cause.

questions.text: The challenge of poverty is likely to be reduced with higher ________ growth.
questions.explanation: Economic growth creates opportunities and provides the resources needed to invest in human development.
